validationTracks.name: LARE Exam Readiness
validationTracks.description: Landscape Architect Registration Examination — 4 sections required for professional licensure in all 50 states, administered by CLARB (Council of Landscape Architectural Registration Boards)

sections.name: Project & Construction Management
sections.description: Contract administration, project delivery methods, professional practice regulations, codes, and construction observation procedures

sections.name: Inventory & Analysis
sections.description: Site assessment, environmental analysis, data collection, contextual evaluation, and suitability determination

sections.name: Design
sections.description: Design development, master planning, planting design, spatial composition, and design communication

sections.name: Grading, Drainage & Construction Documentation
sections.description: Site grading, stormwater management, construction documents, materials specifications, and technical detailing

validationTracks.name: LAAB Accreditation Standards
validationTracks.description: Landscape Architectural Accreditation Board — 14 student learning outcome areas tracked for program accreditation compliance under LAAB Standard 3

sections.name: 3.A — Professional Values & Ethics
sections.description: Knowledge of professional ethics, social responsibility, HBCU mission integration, and stewardship of natural and cultural resources

sections.name: 3.B.1 — History, Theory & Criticism
sections.description: Understanding of the history of landscape architecture, allied professions, and relevant theoretical frameworks

sections.name: 3.B.2 — Design Process
sections.description: Ability to apply design process from problem definition through schematic design and design development

sections.name: 3.B.3 — Site Design & Implementation
sections.description: Ability to develop detailed site designs integrating program, context, and technical requirements

sections.name: 3.B.4 — Communication & Visualization
sections.description: Proficiency in graphic, written, verbal, and digital communication of design ideas

sections.name: 3.B.5 — Natural Systems
sections.description: Understanding of natural systems including ecology, hydrology, soils, vegetation, and climate

sections.name: 3.B.6 — Plants & Ecosystems
sections.description: Knowledge of plants and ecosystems including identification, ecological function, and design application

sections.name: 3.B.7 — Materials & Construction
sections.description: Knowledge of landscape materials, construction methods, structural principles, and assemblies

sections.name: 3.B.8 — Construction Documentation
sections.description: Ability to produce construction documents including plans, sections, details, and specifications

sections.name: 3.B.9 — Professional Practice
sections.description: Understanding of professional practice including project management, ethics, legal responsibilities, and office operations

sections.name: 3.B.10 — Research & Analysis
sections.description: Ability to conduct research and apply analytical methods to inform design and planning decisions

validationTracks.name: ISLA · Licensure Readiness
validationTracks.description: Interactive Study & Licensure Assistant — Track your LARE exam section readiness and professional internship completion. ISLA maps curriculum courses to LARE exam domains and tracks internship credit.

sections.name: LARE §1 — Project & Construction Mgmt
sections.description: Courses preparing you for LARE Section 1: project management, construction administration, contract documents, and professional ethics.

sections.name: LARE §2 — Inventory & Analysis
sections.description: Courses preparing you for LARE Section 2: site inventory, environmental analysis, ecological assessment, and data synthesis.

sections.name: LARE §3 — Design
sections.description: Courses preparing you for LARE Section 3: design methodology, spatial organization, planting design, and aesthetic judgment.

sections.name: LARE §4 — Grading, Drainage & Construction
sections.description: Courses preparing you for LARE Section 4: site grading, stormwater management, construction documentation, and materials.

sections.name: Professional Internship (IDP)
sections.description: Complete the required professional internship (LDAR 400). Required for LARE exam eligibility and CLARB Intern Development Program documentation.
